publishedItems.description:
Original title: TRX Bulls Target $0.45 Despite Stagnant Momentum at Key $0.32 Resistance
Summary: TRON trades sideways at $0.32 as technical indicators signal indecision, but cross-chain infrastructure developments could fuel a breakout toward $0.45 if bulls can crack overhead resistance with c
Article text: TRON trades sideways at $0.32 as technical indicators signal indecision, but cross-chain infrastructure developments could fuel a breakout toward $0.45 if bulls can crack overhead resistance with c...

TRON's positioning in the cross-chain ecosystem continues gaining traction as blockchain interoperability becomes the next major DeFi battleground. The network's focus on becoming a settlement layer for multi-chain applications puts TRX at the center of growing institutional demand for reliable bridging solutions.

Current price action around $0.32 reflects this transitional period. The token has established a solid floor above $0.30 while facing persistent resistance at the $0.33 level. This consolidation pattern typically precedes directional moves, especially when fundamental catalysts align with technical setups.

The indicator constellation suggests coiled energy waiting for a catalyst. Momentum oscillators show neither oversold nor overbought conditions, creating space for upward movement if buying pressure materializes. Moving averages have converged into a tight cluster around current prices, forming the classic launching pad formation that technical traders recognize as high-probability setup territory.

Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.

Volume patterns reveal accumulation behavior rather than distribution. Smart money appears to be methodically building positions without triggering algorithmic momentum systems. This stealth accumulation often precedes sharp moves once retail participation increases.

The options market shows increasing call activity in the $0.35-$0.40 strike range, suggesting institutional players are positioning for upside through year-end. Perpetual swap funding has turned slightly negative, indicating shorts are paying longs to hold positions - typically a contrarian bullish signal when combined with technical consolidation.

Large holder activity remains constructive. Wallet analysis shows continued accumulation among addresses holding 1M+ TRX tokens, while exchange inflows have decreased meaningfully over the past two weeks. This combination typically precedes supply squeezes in mid-cap altcoins.

Primary Scenario (70% probability): TRX breaks above $0.33 resistance within the next two weeks, targeting the $0.38-$0.42 range initially. A sustained move above $0.40 opens the door to $0.45 by December, representing a 40% gain from current levels. The cross-chain narrative provides fundamental support for this trajectory.

Alternative Scenario (30% probability): Failure to clear $0.33 leads to a retest of the $0.30 support zone. However, the accumulation pattern suggests any dip would find aggressive buying interest, making sustained weakness unlikely unless broader crypto markets deteriorate significantly.

The risk-reward profile favors bulls at current levels. Stop losses below $0.30 limit downside to 6% while upside targets offer 25-40% potential returns. TRON's infrastructure positioning in the expanding cross-chain ecosystem provides the fundamental backdrop for sustained appreciation through 2026's first half.

publishedItems.title: ZAMA Collapse to $0.025 Within 72 Hours as Technical Structure Implodes | ZAMA's broken momentum and negative funding rates guarantee a brutal 20% crash to $0.025... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260415-prediction-zama-collapse-to-0025-within-72-hours
publishedItems.description:
Original title: ZAMA Collapse to $0.025 Within 72 Hours as Technical Structure Implodes
Summary: ZAMA's broken momentum and negative funding rates guarantee a brutal 20% crash to $0.025 as overleveraged longs face systematic liquidation across all timeframes.
Article text: ZAMA's broken momentum and negative funding rates guarantee a brutal 20% crash to $0.025 as overleveraged longs face systematic liquidation across all timeframes.

ZAMA is disintegrating in real-time, hemorrhaging 18% in the past 24 hours after failing catastrophically at the $0.04 resistance zone. The token now trades at $0.03 with every technical pillar supporting the recent rally completely shattered.

The momentum structure has collapsed entirely. What started as healthy buying pressure transformed into desperate distribution as early investors dumped their positions into retail FOMO. The RSI surge above 68 combined with price stretching 5% beyond the upper Bollinger Band created the perfect exit liquidity for smart money - and they took it.

The futures market is painting a picture of systematic destruction. Open interest collapsed 32% as overleveraged positions got obliterated, while funding rates flipped negative to -0.0342%. When shorts get paid to hold their positions, it signals institutional money is positioning for further downside.

These aren't temporary dislocations - they represent a fundamental shift in market structure. The combination of negative funding and cratering open interest means the marginal buyer has disappeared. Without fresh leverage entering the system, gravity takes over.

ZAMA's price action broke below the critical 7-day moving average at $0.03, transforming former support into resistance. The MACD histogram flatlined at zero, confirming momentum has completely stalled after exhausting itself on the recent pump attempt.

The stochastic oscillator rolled over from overbought levels with %K at 43 and %D at 35, confirming sellers have regained control of the auction process. More importantly, the 20-day moving average sits at exactly $0.025 - perfectly aligned with the next major support zone from previous consolidation periods.

Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.

The positioning data reveals why this collapse will be particularly vicious. Retail traders maintain 59.4% long exposure while institutional accounts show even higher bullish positioning at 63.3%. This crowded long positioning creates the exact conditions for a liquidity cascade.

When everyone expects the same outcome, the market typically delivers the opposite. The universal bullish sentiment means no natural buyers exist at current levels - every bounce will be met with fresh selling pressure as trapped longs scramble for exits.

ZAMA will test $0.025 within 72 hours as technical selling accelerates. Any bounce toward $0.032-$0.033 represents a premium short entry against the broken moving average resistance. The first wave of selling targets $0.027, but the real destruction happens on the continuation move to $0.025.

The 20% decline to $0.025 isn't a prediction - it's an inevitability based on market structure. With negative funding rates, collapsed open interest, and broken technical levels, ZAMA faces a liquidity vacuum that only major support at $0.025 can fill. The only question is how fast it gets there.

publishedItems.title: SOL Drops to $75 Before April Rally - Short-Term Pain, Medium-Term Gain | Solana's current $83 level won't hold as whale distribution patterns point toward $75-77... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260415-prediction-sol-drops-to-75-before-april-rally
publishedItems.description:
Original title: SOL Drops to $75 Before April Rally - Short-Term Pain, Medium-Term Gain
Summary: Solana's current $83 level won't hold as whale distribution patterns point toward $75-77 support within days. This pullback sets up the launch pad for SOL's push toward $95 by early May.
Article text: Solana's current $83 level won't hold as whale distribution patterns point toward $75-77 support within days. This pullback sets up the launch pad for SOL's push toward $95 by early May.

Solana's 3.34% drop to $83.16 isn't random market noise - it's the inevitable result of momentum dying at resistance. The broader crypto market lacks conviction, with Bitcoin stalling near $71k and institutions rotating profits rather than adding fresh capital.

Volume tells the real story. At $280 million daily, we're seeing steady institutional participation, but the buying pressure that drove previous rallies has evaporated. Smart money is stepping back, waiting for cheaper entry points.

SOL is trapped in a descending wedge between the 7-day moving average resistance at $84 and weakening support at $82.59. The RSI at 47.85 shows neither oversold conditions that trigger buying nor momentum that sustains rallies.

More concerning is SOL's position within its recent trading range. The coin sits at just 58% of its Bollinger Band width - not oversold enough to attract bargain hunters, but weak enough to break lower on any selling pressure.

The next logical support sits at $79.47, but the real buyer interest won't emerge until the $75-77 zone where previous accumulation occurred. That's a 9-12% drop from current levels - painful but necessary to flush out weak hands.

Despite the bearish price action, large traders maintain a 3:1 long bias with 75% positioned bullish. This isn't blind optimism - these players understand that temporary weakness creates better entry opportunities for the next leg higher.

The negative funding rate of -0.0091% means shorts are paying longs to hold their positions, indicating oversold conditions in derivatives markets. Meanwhile, open interest climbing 2.41% to $807 million while price declines suggests accumulation during weakness.

SOL breaks $82.59 support within 48 hours, triggering stops and algorithmic selling toward the $75-77 zone. This isn't capitulation - it's profit-taking rotation that creates the foundation for the next rally.

publishedItems.title: ETH Eyes $2,500 Breakout as Technical Setup Aligns for Weekly Rally | Ethereum consolidates above key support with momentum indicators primed for continuation.... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260415-prediction-eth-eyes-2500-breakout-as-technical-setup
publishedItems.description:
Original title: ETH Eyes $2,500 Breakout as Technical Setup Aligns for Weekly Rally
Summary: Ethereum consolidates above key support with momentum indicators primed for continuation. Bulls control the narrative as buying pressure dominates, setting up a probable push toward $2,500 within s
Article text: Ethereum consolidates above key support with momentum indicators primed for continuation. Bulls control the narrative as buying pressure dominates, setting up a probable push toward $2,500 within s...

Ethereum trades in textbook consolidation mode at $2,320, holding firmly above the critical 50-day moving average while the 20-day provides dynamic support. This isn't random sideways action - it's institutional accumulation disguised as boredom.

The momentum picture tells the real story. While retail traders fixate on short-term noise, the underlying technical structure screams continuation. The RSI maintains healthy positioning without showing exhaustion signals, and the MACD continues displaying bullish characteristics that preceded ETH's strongest rallies this cycle.

What separates this setup from typical consolidation? The buying pressure never stopped. Aggressive market orders consistently favor the bid, creating the type of foundation that launches explosive moves when resistance finally cracks.

The options and futures markets reveal smart money positioning. Open interest expansion without corresponding funding rate stress indicates sophisticated players are building positions for a directional move. When institutional flow dominates without retail overleveraging, the resulting moves tend to be both powerful and sustainable.

The key resistance zone sits between $2,380-$2,390, representing the convergence of technical levels and psychological barriers. Breaking through this ceiling opens the path toward $2,450, with minimal resistance until the major zone around $2,500.

Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.

ETH's technical roadmap suggests a breakout sequence within the next 48-72 hours. The consolidation pattern typically resolves in favor of the prevailing trend, and momentum indicators align with continuation rather than reversal.

The high-probability scenario unfolds as follows: Initial break above $2,390 triggers algorithmic buying, pushing price toward $2,450 by midweek. From there, sustained momentum carries ETH toward the $2,500 psychological level within seven days, assuming Bitcoin maintains neutral to positive price action.

This isn't hopeful speculation - it's pattern recognition based on how similar setups have resolved throughout ETH's trading history. The combination of technical positioning, momentum characteristics, and derivative market structure creates a compelling case for upward resolution.

The downside scenario requires a breakdown below $2,277 support, which would invalidate the bullish thesis and open the door toward $2,200 retests. However, current market structure suggests this outcome carries roughly 30% probability compared to the 70% chance of upward breakout.

Position accordingly, but recognize that ETH's current technical picture favors patient bulls over anxious bears.

publishedItems.title: Bitcoin Eyes $80K Run as Technical Setup Aligns at $74K | Bitcoin's consolidation above $74K support sets up a potential breakout toward $80,000, w... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260415-prediction-bitcoin-eyes-80k-run-as-technical-setup
publishedItems.description:
Original title: Bitcoin Eyes $80K Run as Technical Setup Aligns at $74K
Summary: Bitcoin's consolidation above $74K support sets up a potential breakout toward $80,000, with key resistance at $77K standing as the final hurdle before the next major leg higher.
Article text: Bitcoin's consolidation above $74K support sets up a potential breakout toward $80,000, with key resistance at $77K standing as the final hurdle before the next major leg higher.

Bitcoin trades at $73,949 after establishing support above the 50-day EMA at $69,684. The price action shows classic accumulation patterns, with buyers stepping in at each dip while sellers struggle to push below critical support levels.

The RSI reading of 60.30 indicates momentum remains positive without reaching overbought territory. This leaves room for continued upward movement, particularly as the indicator hasn't approached the typical reversal zones above 70.

Bollinger Bands show Bitcoin riding near the upper band at 0.87, suggesting continued bullish pressure. The bands haven't expanded dramatically, indicating the current move maintains controlled momentum rather than parabolic acceleration that often leads to sharp reversals.

The MACD histogram sits near zero, reflecting a neutral short-term momentum state. Rather than signaling weakness, this positioning often precedes significant directional moves as buyers and sellers reach equilibrium before the next impulse.

The resistance cluster between $75,487 and $77,024 represents the primary obstacle for bulls. Breaking through this zone with sustained volume would eliminate significant selling pressure and open a clear path toward $80,000.

Trading volume of $1.81 billion on Binance spot reflects genuine market participation rather than low-volume drift. The taker buy/sell ratio at 1.27 shows aggressive buying continues to outpace selling pressure, indicating institutional accumulation during recent price consolidation.

Open interest declined 5.55% over the past 24 hours, suggesting leveraged weak hands exited positions during the pullback to $73,514. This reduction in speculative positioning typically precedes more sustainable price movements.

Retail traders maintain only 42.8% long exposure while larger participants hold 44.4% long positions. This positioning creates potential fuel for upward moves, as retail sentiment often contrasts with price direction during trend continuations.

The relatively balanced positioning among sophisticated traders suggests they're prepared for movement in either direction but haven't committed heavily to short positions that could create squeeze conditions.

Bitcoin's technical setup supports a move toward $80,000 within the next two weeks if current support levels hold. The primary catalyst would be a decisive break above $77,000 with accompanying volume expansion.

Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.

The most likely scenario involves Bitcoin testing the $77K resistance zone multiple times before achieving a breakout. Once this level falls, minimal technical resistance exists until $80,000, potentially allowing for rapid price appreciation.

A failure to hold support at $72,900 would shift the near-term outlook negative and likely trigger a retest of the $69,700-$70,500 zone. However, the broader technical structure suggests any such decline would attract significant buying interest.

The confluence of technical indicators, market structure, and positioning data supports a bullish bias over the next 30 days. Bitcoin appears positioned for another leg higher, with $80,000 representing a realistic target if current momentum patterns persist.

Bitcoin trades at $73,949 after establishing support above the 50-day EMA at $69,684. The price action shows classic accumulation patterns, with buyers stepping in at each dip while sellers struggle to push below critical support levels.

The RSI reading of 60.30 indicates momentum remains positive without reaching overbought territory. This leaves room for continued upward movement, particularly as the indicator hasn't approached the typical reversal zones above 70.

Bollinger Bands show Bitcoin riding near the upper band at 0.87, suggesting continued bullish pressure. The bands haven't expanded dramatically, indicating the current move maintains controlled momentum rather than parabolic acceleration that often leads to sharp reversals.

The MACD histogram sits near zero, reflecting a neutral short-term momentum state. Rather than signaling weakness, this positioning often precedes significant directional moves as buyers and sellers reach equilibrium before the next impulse.

The resistance cluster between $75,487 and $77,024 represents the primary obstacle for bulls. Breaking through this zone with sustained volume would eliminate significant selling pressure and open a clear path toward $80,000.

Trading volume of $1.81 billion on Binance spot reflects genuine market participation rather than low-volume drift. The taker buy/sell ratio at 1.27 shows aggressive buying continues to outpace selling pressure, indicating institutional accumulation during recent price consolidation.

Open interest declined 5.55% over the past 24 hours, suggesting leveraged weak hands exited positions during the pullback to $73,514. This reduction in speculative positioning typically precedes more sustainable price movements.

Retail traders maintain only 42.8% long exposure while larger participants hold 44.4% long positions. This positioning creates potential fuel for upward moves, as retail sentiment often contrasts with price direction during trend continuations.

The relatively balanced positioning among sophisticated traders suggests they're prepared for movement in either direction but haven't committed heavily to short positions that could create squeeze conditions.

Bitcoin's technical setup supports a move toward $80,000 within the next two weeks if current support levels hold. The primary catalyst would be a decisive break above $77,000 with accompanying volume expansion.

Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.

The most likely scenario involves Bitcoin testing the $77K resistance zone multiple times before achieving a breakout. Once this level falls, minimal technical resistance exists until $80,000, potentially allowing for rapid price appreciation.

A failure to hold support at $72,900 would shift the near-term outlook negative and likely trigger a retest of the $69,700-$70,500 zone. However, the broader technical structure suggests any such decline would attract significant buying interest.

The confluence of technical indicators, market structure, and positioning data supports a bullish bias over the next 30 days. Bitcoin appears positioned for another leg higher, with $80,000 representing a realistic target if current momentum patterns persist.

publishedItems.title: Visa Becomes Anchor Validator on Tempo Blockchain for Stablecoin Payments | Visa launches in-house validator node on Stripe's Tempo blockchain, joining anchor valida... https://Blockchain.News/news/visa-anchor-validator-tempo-blockchain-stablecoin-payments
publishedItems.description:
Original title: Visa Becomes Anchor Validator on Tempo Blockchain for Stablecoin Payments
Summary: Visa launches in-house validator node on Stripe's Tempo blockchain, joining anchor validators for stablecoin settlement as payment giants race to build crypto infrastructure.
Article text: Visa launches in-house validator node on Stripe's Tempo blockchain, joining anchor validators for stablecoin settlement as payment giants race to build crypto infrastructure.

Visa has officially entered the transaction validation business. The payments giant launched an in-house validator node on Tempo, the Layer 1 blockchain co-developed by Stripe and Paradigm, marking its deepest operational commitment to blockchain infrastructure yet.

The node, developed over six months with Tempo's engineering team, positions Visa as an "anchor validator" alongside Stripe and Zodia Custody. Unlike simply integrating blockchain payments, Visa is now directly verifying and processing transactions on a network built specifically for real-time stablecoin settlement.

Tempo isn't another general-purpose blockchain. Launched to mainnet in March 2026, it's purpose-built for payments with some notable design choices that appeal to traditional finance players.

Transaction fees target one-tenth of a cent—paid directly in USD stablecoins rather than a native gas token. That eliminates a major friction point for institutions that don't want treasury exposure to volatile crypto assets. The network claims over 100,000 transactions per second with sub-second finality, specs that actually matter for payment processing at scale.

The validator role isn't purely symbolic. Validators on Tempo earn stablecoin-denominated rewards when selected to package transactions into blocks, giving Visa direct economic participation in network activity.

Visa's move reflects a broader pattern: major payment companies aren't just accepting crypto—they're building the infrastructure.

Stripe acquired stablecoin platform Bridge for $1.1 billion in October 2024, then launched stablecoin accounts for businesses in over 100 countries. Mastercard followed with its $1.8 billion acquisition of BVNK last month, gaining stablecoin payment capabilities across 130 countries.

Visa has taken a different approach, preferring to build rather than buy. The company expanded its settlement platform last July to support PayPal USD and Euro Coin across networks including Stellar and Avalanche. It also runs a validator on the Canton Network for privacy-focused institutional payments.

The Tempo validator adds direct blockchain operations to that portfolio. Visa is no longer just a bridge between crypto and traditional payments—it's becoming embedded in the transaction layer itself.

The infrastructure buildout comes as stablecoin adoption accelerates. Total market cap reached nearly $319 billion at publication, up from $307.5 billion at the start of 2026—a roughly $11.5 billion increase in under four months.

Tempo's design attracted input from an unusual coalition during development: Mastercard, UBS, Deutsche Bank, OpenAI, and Shopify all contributed to the blockchain's specifications. That kind of institutional involvement before mainnet launch suggests serious enterprise interest in stablecoin-native payment rails.

Visa's expanded stablecoin card partnership with Bridge now covers 18 countries, with plans to reach over 100 markets by year-end. The Tempo validator gives the company operational presence on yet another network where those transactions might eventually settle.

For traders watching institutional adoption metrics, Visa running its own blockchain infrastructure represents a different category of commitment than partnership announcements or pilot programs. The six-month development timeline and in-house operation signal this isn't a press release play.

publishedItems.title: Harvey AI Processes 700K Daily Legal Tasks as Agentic AI Reshapes Law | Legal AI startup Harvey reveals its platform handles 700,000+ daily tasks and extracts 50... https://Blockchain.News/news/harvey-ai-700k-daily-legal-tasks-agentic-platform
publishedItems.description:
Original title: Harvey AI Processes 700K Daily Legal Tasks as Agentic AI Reshapes Law
Summary: Legal AI startup Harvey reveals its platform handles 700,000+ daily tasks and extracts 50 million contract terms weekly as agentic AI transforms legal work.
Article text: Legal AI startup Harvey reveals its platform handles 700,000+ daily tasks and extracts 50 million contract terms weekly as agentic AI transforms legal work.

Legal AI startup Harvey has quietly built one of the most active enterprise AI deployments in the market, processing over 700,000 tasks daily and extracting more than 50 million contract terms every week. The company disclosed these figures while outlining its vision for agentic AI in legal work.

The numbers matter because they represent real production workloads, not pilot programs or demos. While much of the AI industry debates what "agentic" actually means, Harvey claims it's been running this way for some time—and the throughput data suggests enterprise clients are actually using it.

Harvey's approach splits legal work into distinct categories requiring different AI treatment. Repeatable tasks—contract review checklists, compliance checks, regulatory monitoring—get handled by agents following structured steps with zero variance. Think factory floor precision applied to document processing.

Complex work operates differently. For multi-step matters like full transaction analysis or fund formation lifecycle management, Harvey's agents generate a plan that lawyers review before execution begins. The system logs routine decisions but pauses for human input on anything outside the playbook or materially changing direction.

This isn't AI replacing lawyers. It's AI handling the execution while lawyers provide judgment on the hard calls. The distinction matters for enterprise adoption—law firms and corporate legal teams need audit trails and human oversight baked in, not bolted on.

Foundation models crossed a threshold. They can now maintain coherence across longer tasks and produce consistent outputs with less variance than even a year ago. Harvey had already built infrastructure for this kind of execution through features like Deep Analysis. The models finally caught up to what the architecture could support.

For enterprise software investors and legal tech watchers, Harvey's scale provides a benchmark. Processing 50 million terms weekly suggests deep integration with client workflows, not surface-level adoption. That kind of stickiness typically translates to strong retention metrics.

The company positions its platform as a "legal operating system"—ambitious framing that implies becoming the default layer between lawyers and their work product. Whether that vision pans out depends on whether the 700,000 daily tasks keep growing and whether competitors can match the execution quality at similar scale.

Harvey says it's building toward agents that compound knowledge over time, getting smarter the longer clients use them. That's the real moat play: not just processing volume, but learning from it.

Original title: Anthropic Launches Claude Code Routines for Automated Development Workflows
Summary: Claude Code's new routines feature enables scheduled and event-driven AI automations for code review, bug fixes, and deployment verification without local infrastructure.
Article text: Claude Code's new routines feature enables scheduled and event-driven AI automations for code review, bug fixes, and deployment verification without local infrastructure.

Anthropic has rolled out a research preview of routines for Claude Code, allowing developers to configure AI-powered automations that run on schedules, respond to API calls, or trigger from GitHub events—all without keeping a laptop open or managing custom infrastructure.

The feature addresses a friction point that's plagued developer automation: managing cron jobs, servers, and tooling just to get an AI assistant to handle repetitive tasks. Routines bundle prompts, repository access, and connectors into packages that execute on Anthropic's web infrastructure.

Scheduled routines work on hourly, nightly, or weekly cadences. A team could configure one to pull the top bug from Linear at 2am, attempt a fix, and open a draft PR before anyone wakes up. Developers already using the /schedule command in Claude Code's CLI will find those tasks automatically converted to scheduled routines.

API routines get their own endpoints and auth tokens. POST a message, get back a session URL. This opens integration possibilities with alerting systems, deployment pipelines, and internal tools. One use case already emerging: Datadog alerts trigger a routine that pulls traces, correlates them with recent deployments, and drafts a fix before on-call engineers even open the page.

Webhook routines—starting with GitHub—subscribe to repository events. Claude spins up a session for each matching PR and continues monitoring for comments and CI failures. Teams are using this for automated code review against custom checklists and even cross-language library ports, where a Python SDK change automatically generates a matching Go SDK PR.

Beta users have gravitated toward a few workflows. Nightly backlog triage labels and assigns new issues, then posts summaries to Slack. Weekly docs-drift scans catch documentation that references changed APIs and open update PRs automatically. Deploy verification routines run smoke checks and scan error logs, posting go/no-go decisions to release channels.

The feedback-to-fix pipeline stands out: a docs feedback widget sends reports directly to a routine, which opens a session with the relevant repo and drafts changes without human intervention on the initial pass.

Routines are live now for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers with Claude Code on the web enabled. The daily caps: Pro gets 5 routine runs, Max gets 15, and Team/Enterprise users get 25. Additional runs consume extra usage allocation. All routine execution draws from standard subscription limits, same as interactive sessions.

Anthropic says webhook triggers will expand beyond GitHub to additional event sources, though no timeline was provided. For developers tired of maintaining automation infrastructure, the pitch is straightforward: define the task once, let it run in the cloud.
